item.title: Possible flesh-eating screwworm case in Texas, USDA says
item.description: A possible case of the flesh-eating New World screwworm is being investigated in Texas, the USDA reported Wednesday.

item.title: Flesh-eating New World screwworm found in Texas calf, USDA says
item.description: The USDA said the only animal affected was a 3-week-old calf in Zavala County, Texas, after larvae were identified in its umbilical area.

item.title: Iran attacks Kuwait airport as Israel, Lebanon reach tentative ceasefire
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed Wednesday to renew their fragile ceasefire and create a number of "pilot" security zones inside Lebanon where Hezbollah would be banned.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to renew ceasefire if Hezbollah cuts off attacks
item.description: The Israeli and Lebanese governments have agreed to implement a ceasefire, after weeks of deadly fighting between Israel and Hezbollah had imperiled broader negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end their conflict.

item.title: ChatGPT user says, "anyone could get engaged with relational chatbots"
item.description: CBS News spoke with five people who say they were led to believe they had developed an emotional connection to an AI chatbot. They are now involved in a digital support group for people who say they experienced AI-fueled delusions or spirals. ChatGPT user Micky Small joins "The Daily Report" to share her experience.

item.title: U.S.-Iran tensions on rise as Iran strikes Kuwait airport
item.description: Over the past 24 hours, the U.S. and Iran have executed some of the most intense fighting since the ceasefire went into effect, and unlike other cases where military targets were hit, a civilian area came under fire. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Where things stand in California after Tuesday's primaries
item.description: Election results from California's primary races were still coming in on Wednesday. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto has the latest on the races.

item.title: Brooklyn video store sees new demand for VHS tapes, DVDs
item.description: A Brooklyn video store is going old school and it's drawing a new generation of movie fans. Evyn Moon shows how DVD and VHS are making a comeback as people trade algorithms for aisle browsing.

item.title: North Korea announces opening of a new nuclear facility
item.description: State media photos showed what appeared to be a large centrifuge hall, indicating the plant is likely used to enrich weapons-grade uranium.

item.title: 6/3: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: House passes measure that would rein in Trump on Iran; Trump says Iran talks are ongoing despite recent attack.

item.title: How AI is upending the fashion industry... and how students are adapting
item.description: New York City has long been considered the fashion capital of the world, but fashion jobs have declined 30% over the past decade. Evyn Moon visited New York's Fashion Institute of Technology to find out how future generations are reinventing the industry in the AI era.

item.title: Hostage situation at bank building in California ends after suspect shot dead
item.description: The suspect was pronounced dead at the scene early Wednesday morning, the Bakersfield Police Department said.

item.title: House votes to rein in Trump on Iran as war loses GOP support
item.description: In May, the Senate advanced a similar measure to force the president to end the prolonged conflict with Iran.

item.title: CBS News fires longtime 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley
item.description: CBS News has fired longtime 60 Minutes correspondent and former "CBS Evening News" anchor Scott Pelley one day after he had a tense and confrontational exchange with new 60 Minutes executive producer Nick Bilton.

item.title: Inside the new yoga workout with swords: "WeaponUp"
item.description: Take martial arts, yoga, and a sword and you get a fitness craze that's gone viral online and is starting to gain traction in person. Jarred Hill reports.

item.title: Middle school gym teacher breaks basketball records, awing his students
item.description: The NBA Finals tip off on Wednesday, and viewers can expect lots of scoring. But in Maine, a middle school teacher was able to set the Guinness World Record for most three-pointers, most free throws and most half-court shots in an hour. On Monday, he worked to break another record. Tony Dokoupil has the story of "Dr. Buckets."

item.title: Special Coast Guard team searching waters for Lynette Hooker case evidence
item.description: A team of specialized Coast Guard divers and investigators have been searching the Sea of Abaco in the Bahamas for missing Michigan mother Lynette Hooker. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: CBS News editor-in-chief addresses Scott Pelley's firing
item.description: Scott Pelley, a 60 Minutes correspondent and former anchor of the "CBS Evening News," was fired on Tuesday. Jim Axelrod and Tony Dokoupil have more.

item.title: Pence's former chief of staff warns Iran war threatens farmer vote for GOP
item.description: A new op-ed in the Washington Post suggests the war in Iran and President Trump's trade policy risk Republicans losing the support of a key coalition: farmers. Republican strategist Marc Short, who wrote the piece, and Democratic strategist Mo Elleithee join with analysis.

item.title: 6/3: CBS Evening News
item.description: FBI kills hostage-taker in California after 15-hour standoff; President Trump declines to say whether he's dropped "anti-weaponization" fund plans.

item.title: Rep. Joyce Beatty lauds passage of Iran war powers resolution, judge's order on Kennedy Center name
item.description: A federal judge last week ordered the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center and also barred the performing arts center from closing its doors for proposed renovations. Democratic Rep. Joyce Beatty of Ohio, who brought the lawsuit and also serves on the board of trustees, joins to discuss that and Wednesday's House passage of an Iran war powers resolution.

item.title: Victim of "horrifying" Chelsea murder identified after 26 years
item.description: "Chelsea Jane Doe" has been identified as Tiffany Bradley of Pennsylvania.

item.title: NASA reluctantly gives up on lost orbiter: "Best Mars mission ever"
item.description: NASA officials said the $582 million MAVEN orbiter could not be recovered after a problem on the far side of Mars late last year, and that its extraordinarily successful mission was at an end.

item.title: American Airlines suspends 6 routes because of high jet fuel costs
item.description: American Airlines said it is temporarily cutting six routes amid rising jet fuel costs, including several flights to Los Angeles.

item.title: Ford recalls nearly 420,000 vehicles over faulty seat belts
item.description: Safety watchdog said seat belts in certain Ford Expedition and Lincoln vehicles may inadvertently lock, preventing them from functioning properly.

item.title: Scientists find yeast in frozen mummy's guts, use it to make bread
item.description: More than 5,300 years ago, Oetzi the Iceman was strolling through the Alps on the border of Austria and Italy when he was killed by an arrow in the back.

item.title: Teen released by ICE wants to stay in U.S.: "Doing the right things"
item.description: Ricardo Hernandez-Navarrete graduated from high school after being released by ICE, but he and his mother still face the possibility of deportation.

item.title: Ralph the dog's impact on science and his family
item.description: The Rowe family dog Ralph was one of many canines with dementia who participated in a study of rapamycin. Scientists were able to gain new insight into the drug's potential as a treatment by studying his brain.

item.title: Comparative Oncology | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: Humans share many of the same genes as dogs. In 2022, Anderson Cooper reported on how scientists were using that similarity in a field called comparative oncology, testing new cancer treatments on dogs and humans.

item.title: Attack on the Judiciary | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: In 2021, 60 Minutes spoke with federal judge Esther Salas, who was in her New Jersey home when a gunman targeting her opened fire, wounding her husband and killing her son. Judge Salas told Bill Whitaker about her fight for better protection for judges in the face of mounting violence.
